message: ⚠️ FLAG: Meridian Freight - Driver #38 (Carlos Mendez) - 3 fuel transactions within 45 min at locations 80mi apart. Transaction amounts: $412, $387, $218. Auto-held card pending manual review. Risk score for this driver session: 78/100.
resolution: Confirmed with merchant - GPS coordinates were incorrect on the POS system at the Anniston, AL location. Legitimate transactions. Releasing hold.

message: Meridian Freight asking for line increase from $185K to $250K again. Mike Davis is a great customer but getting impatient with our turnaround time. He mentioned WEX is courting them. Can we fast-track the review?
resolution: On it. Will call Mike today with a 3-day turnaround commitment.

message: 🚨 ALERT: QuickHaul Logistics - Utilization spike to 70.7% (expected: ~40%). Spend velocity 74% above model prediction for fleet size. 12 transactions at unidentified merchant (ID#449281) totaling $8,900 in 5 days. Flagging for IMMEDIATE manual review.
resolution: If docs don't come by Friday EOD, let's reduce the line to $25K and put a temporary hold on new card issuance. I'd rather be safe here.

message: QuickHaul needs full review. Summary of flags: (1) 70.7% utilization in month 2, (2) $8.9K at unknown merchant, (3) 18 cards for 12 trucks, (4) off-hours transactions, (5) late payment in month 1, (6) owner evasive on docs. Anyone seen the FMCSA data? Their DOT# is 4102847.
resolution: Copy. Blocking new card requests now. Already had 6 on hold.

message: 🎉 Greenfield renewing annual contract! QBR went great with Linda Park. Big news: they're acquiring Midwest Carriers (~30 trucks). Could be +$60K MRR for us. Linda wants a dedicated onboarding plan for the acquisition. Also requesting API integration and volume discount conversation.
resolution: Perfect. Also flagging - Linda mentioned their board wants 15% cost reduction across all vendors. We should proactively build a Coast ROI report before renewal negotiation goes further.

message: Feature request from Patriot Plumbing (COAST-6743): They want per-job spending limits that sync with BuildOps job IDs. Currently they're manually reconciling job costs. Dave says this would save them another 5+ hrs/month on top of the 10 hrs they already save with the integration. This could be a big differentiator for field service accounts.
resolution: Will schedule a call with Dave and the product team next week.
